name: JonDash official addons
description: Official add-on modules and helpers for JonDash. In JonDash, add this repo as a module source (Admin -> Modules -> Manage sources) to browse and install. Modules update independently of the JonDash base app. Channels: the main branch = stable; this manifest (beta branch) = beta. See VERSIONING.md.

modules.name: Health monitoring
modules.description: Watches your services with HTTP, TCP, ping, DNS and certificate checks, records uptime and response times, and alerts by email or webhook when something goes down.
modules.notes: Housekeeping only — monitoring behaves exactly as before. Corrects the JonDash version this module says it needs, which named the release where it should have named the pre-release, and repairs a shipped test that no longer compiled against JonDash 1.5.2.

modules.name: Module template (for developers)
modules.description: FOR DEVELOPERS — a working example to copy when building your own module. Installs to modules/template; open MODULE.md in that folder for a full guide, and AI-PROMPT.md to have an AI build one for you. Safe to install, and safe to uninstall when you're done.
modules.notes: The template depends on nothing: no helpers and no background work, so copying it gives you a module that installs on its own. Adds a done toggle — showing a follow-up migration and a permission the code actually uses — and a fuller guide, including a paste-in AI prompt for building a module.

modules.name: Backup Manager
modules.description: Keeps folders copied to another location — a network share or an external drive — on a schedule, and tells you what it did. Can keep dated copies and tidy old ones away automatically.
modules.notes: Fixes the dashboard tile, which drew no card of its own — so instead of matching the other tiles it appeared as loose text on the dashboard background, with nothing naming which module it belonged to. It now looks like the rest, with a title and a link into the module.

helpers.name: Scheduler
helpers.description: Runs modules' declared background work on time, starting when the server starts rather than when someone opens a page.
helpers.notes: Corrects the JonDash version this helper says it needs, which named the release where it should have named the pre-release. The helper itself is unchanged.

helpers.name: Files and folders
helpers.description: Lets a module copy and archive folders to another location — a network share, an external drive — within the folders you allow. JonDash's own secrets are never copied.

provides.label: Look at files and folders in the locations you allow
Create and change files in the locations you allow
Delete files in the locations you allow

helpers.notes: A documentation fix only — the helper itself is unchanged. Its claim that every copy removed by retention reaches the audit trail holds on JonDash 1.5.3 and later, and never did before it. It now says so, and what still holds on older versions: the log records every removal either way.
